skill.name: py-testing-async
skill.description: Guides Python teams through reliable async tests with pytest-asyncio, async fixtures, database isolation, AsyncMock, and FastAPI client testing.
skill.summary: Write reliable pytest-asyncio tests for async services, databases, HTTP clients, and error flows.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are documentation false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code, and pytest examples. The only real operational guidance is user-visible pytest command examples, so the residual risk is low and the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown code fences, inline code, and pytest command examples. No Ruby shell execution or hidden command invocation is present in the skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are documentation examples in SKILL.md, not executable skill code. The only shell examples are explicit pytest commands for user-run test workflows.

low_findings.title: Local Test URL Is Not Network Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL at line 171 is the httpx AsyncClient base URL "http://test" used with ASGITransport. This is an in-process test convention, not an external network endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The same example configures ASGITransport with the local app, which means requests are routed in process. No evidence found of data being sent to an outside host.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The lines flagged for weak cryptography describe async testing concepts. They do not contain MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text contains the word async and test descriptions, with no cryptographic operation. No evidence found of weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance detections are ordinary test sample names, fixture scopes, and query examples. They do not enumerate host data, interfaces, ports, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows database IDs, fixture scope descriptions, and parameter names. No evidence found of commands or instructions for host or network discovery.

content.value_statement: Async Python tests often fail because fixtures, event loops, mocks, and database transactions need special handling. This skill provides practical pytest-asyncio patterns for reliable async service, API, and database tests.
actual_capabilities: Explains pytest-asyncio strict and auto configuration patterns.
Shows async fixture patterns for sessions, test data, and cleanup.
Guides database test isolation with transactions and rollback.
Demonstrates AsyncMock usage for async services and patched functions.
Covers FastAPI endpoint testing with httpx AsyncClient and ASGITransport.
Lists pytest commands for running targeted, verbose, and coverage test runs.
limitations: Provides guidance only; it does not run tests automatically.
Examples assume pytest, pytest-asyncio, and common Python async libraries.
Database examples need adaptation for each project schema and fixture setup.
It does not replace project-specific debugging or dependency configuration.

use_cases.title: Add async endpoint tests
use_cases.description: Create reliable FastAPI tests that use an async HTTP client and application transport.

use_cases.title: Stabilize database test suites
use_cases.description: Design async session fixtures that isolate state and clean up after each test.

use_cases.title: Mock async dependencies correctly
use_cases.description: Replace fragile synchronous mocks with AsyncMock patterns for services and side effects.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic async test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple pytest-asyncio example.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a pytest-asyncio test for this async function. Include the marker, arrange-act-assert structure, and the expected assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Design async fixtures
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my async pytest fixtures and suggest a reliable setup for sessions, test data, and cleanup. Explain fixture scope choices.

prompt_templates.title: Fix flaky async database tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for isolation and rollback issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these failing async database tests. Identify where state leaks can happen and propose transaction or rollback fixture changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build an end-to-end async test plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for coverage across a workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a test plan for this async workflow. Cover service functions, API requests, database isolation, error cases, and parameterized cases.

output_examples.input: A service test fails with a coroutine warning and a missing assertion.
output: The response explains that the async call must be awaited.
It recommends adding the pytest async marker or using auto mode.
It suggests assertions for returned values and awaited mock calls.

output_examples.input: A database test suite passes alone but fails when run with other tests.
output: The response identifies shared state as the likely cause.
It recommends function-scoped sessions and rollback after each test.
It explains when a session-scoped engine is still acceptable.

output_examples.input: An API endpoint needs async tests with authenticated requests.
output: The response describes an async client fixture using application transport.
It outlines an authentication header fixture.
It lists success, validation, and error cases to cover.

best_practices: Use function-scoped database sessions when each test needs isolated state.
Use AsyncMock for async collaborators and assert awaited calls explicitly.
Test complete async workflows as well as focused service and error cases.
anti_patterns: Do not use regular Mock for functions that are awaited.
Do not share mutable database state across unrelated tests.
Do not omit pytest-asyncio configuration or markers in strict mode.

faq.question: Does this skill run pytest for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides testing patterns and command examples that a user can choose to run.

faq.question: Which async testing framework does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on pytest with pytest-asyncio for Python async code.

faq.question: Can it help with FastAPI tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes async HTTP client patterns for FastAPI applications.

faq.question: Does it cover database isolation?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains transaction rollback, fixture scopes, and separate test database patterns.

faq.question: Can it help mock async services?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows AsyncMock and patching patterns for awaited dependencies.

faq.question: Is it tied to one application schema?
faq.answer: No. The examples are generic and should be adapted to the project being tested.
